notes: Autonomous eval. Each case is a realistic NextSpark developer task whose CORRECT answer depends on knowledge in the rls-enforcement skill. We compare with-skill vs no-skill (baseline), grade against objective assertions, then improve and re-run to measure pass-rate delta.

evals.name: bootstrap-service-pool
evals.prompt: In @nextsparkjs/core I'm adding a server action `createOrganization(userId, name)` that, in one DB transaction, inserts a new row in `teams` (ownerId = userId), inserts the creator's `team_members` row with role 'owner', and inserts a default `subscriptions` row for the team. The app will run with RLS enabled (DATABASE_URL connects as the non-owner role). Write the data-access code (which db.ts helper do you use and why).

assertions.text: Uses the SERVICE pool (getServiceTransactionClient or { service: true } or a non-userId service path), NOT getTransactionClient(userId)/app pool
Explains the reason: it creates the FIRST team_members/subscription so it cannot satisfy the membership-based RLS insert policy under the user's own GUC
Notes that authorization is enforced at the app/action layer, not by RLS, for this bootstrap

evals.name: bare-query-bypass

assertions.text: Identifies that the bare query()/queryOne()/queryRows() family routes to the SERVICE pool and BYPASSES RLS
Recommends queryWithRLS(sql, params, userId) so the query runs on the app pool with the GUC / RLS evaluated
Frames it as a real risk (RLS not enforced) rather than a style nit

evals.name: team-role-text-extension
evals.prompt: Our theme needs a new team role called 'reviewer'. How do I make it real so it can be stored in team_members.role and recognized by permission checks? Do I need a migration?

assertions.text: States that team_role is TEXT (not a Postgres ENUM), so no ALTER TYPE / no DB migration is needed to add the value
Says to declare the role via config (availableTeamRoles in app.config + permissions.config.ts)
Explicitly warns against ALTER TYPE team_role ADD VALUE

evals.name: policy-primitive-ordering
evals.prompt: I'm hardening the RLS policy on the `account` table by editing migration 002_auth_tables.sql to use `public.can_bypass_rls()`. On a fresh db:reset it fails with: ERROR: function public.can_bypass_rls() does not exist. Why, and how do I fix it without an incremental migration?

assertions.text: Explains the cause: can_bypass_rls (and friends) must exist BEFORE migration 002 runs; defining them later (e.g. 010) is a forward dependency that fails at CREATE POLICY time
Fix: define the primitive in migration 001 (the foundational functions) so 002 can use it
Notes that a SECURITY DEFINER function's table refs resolve at call time, so it can be defined before those tables exist

evals.name: theme-override-coexistence
evals.prompt: My theme ships a phase-2 migration that overrides the core `subscriptions` SELECT policy with a stricter campus-specific one. But after db:reset, a plain team member can still read the team's subscriptions as if the core policy were still active. What's wrong and how do I fix the override?

assertions.text: Explains that RLS combines multiple permissive policies with OR, so the core policy and the theme policy COEXIST (the looser core one still grants access)
Fix: the theme override must DROP the core policy by name — and drop BOTH the legacy AND the current (beta.167) core policy names — before creating its own
Mentions the theme override runs in phase 2 (after core) which is why it must drop the core's policies
